title: Logs, Events, Metrics, Inspect, Debugging, and Incident Evidence · Docker Systems Brief: Docker Exec Debug Toolbox
summary: A service restarts intermittently with no useful application error while disk, memory, network, and health evidence disagree. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker exec debug toolbox increment in layered Docker incident evidence packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose inspect-first evidence, exec, cp, export, temporary toolboxes, debug images, namespace entry, and reproduction without mutating the production image blindly.
Interpret CPU, memory, network, block I/O, PIDs, cgroups, writable-layer size, image store, volume, and host pressure evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

steps.title: Predict Docker Exec Debug Toolbox · Theory Docker Exec Debug Toolbox
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory container exits only after revision 5; an operator restarts it and edits files with exec before collecting state. Reconstruct the first causal layer from inspect, events, logs, stats, and release identity.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Exec Debug Toolbox contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example container exits only after revision 5; an operator restarts it and edits files with exec before collecting state. Reconstruct the first causal layer from inspect, events, logs, stats, and release identity. Competency probe: Choose inspect-first evidence, exec, cp, export, temporary toolboxes, debug images, namespace entry, and reproduction without mutating the production image blindly. A defensible solution applies Docker Exec Debug Toolbox, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to layered Docker incident evidence packet.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-exec-debug-toolbox — Choose inspect-first evidence, exec, cp, export, temporary toolboxes, debug images, namespace entry, and reproduction without mutating the production image blindly.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-observability-debugging-4
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-observability-debugging-4
    artifact: incident-evidence.yaml
    decision: worked docker observability debugging 4
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      baseline: ps-inspect-events-logs-stats
      correlation: release-digest-and-container-id
      mutation-policy: reproduce-before-exec
      diagnostic-image: separately-reviewed
      conclusion: first-causal-layer
    verify: docker inspect failed-api --format json && docker logs --timestamps failed-api
    failure: Reject interactive mutation as diagnosis, unbounded log collection, high-cardinality labels, or a restart presented as root-cause evidence.
    repair: Preserve state and timestamps, compare desired with observed configuration, isolate one layer, reproduce cleanly, and retain the causal regression.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Installing tools into a live container is the safest durable way to debug and repair it. This fails the Docker Exec Debug Toolbox contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.
